Sir Aurel Stein identifies its capital Daratpuri with modern Gurez.
Tnc seats of the Daradas extend from Chitral and Yasin across the
Indus region of Gilgit, Chilas and Bunji to the Kishangahga
valley in the immediate north of Kashmir,
The Tahganas are Ptolemy's Tanganoi whose territory stretch-
ed from the RamgaAga river to the upper Sarayu. They were one of

Division of Globe XIV 183
the aboriginal tribes which the Aryans, while pushing their conqu-
ests to the east of the Gaftga and Yamuna, drove back into the
Himalayas or towards the Vindhyas. The Tonk Rajputs of Rohil-
khand and the Dangayas are said to be the present representatives
of the TaAganas.
Brahmapura is mentioned in the AgnipurSna. Cunningham
identifies it with Variat-pattan on the Ramganga river. Yuan
Chwang mentions it as a country which lay in the Garhwal and
Kumaun region.
